title: lens culinaris subsp. esculenta
intro: Lens culinaris subsp. esculenta, also known as the common or brown lentil, is a subspecies of the lens culinaris plant that is widely cultivated for its edible seeds. The lentil plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of diabetes due to its rich content of fiber, protein, and antioxidants. The health benefits of consuming lentils include a reduced risk of heart disease, improved digestion, and a lower risk of certain cancers, making it a valuable addition to a balanced diet. Herbal preparations of lentil, such as tea and tincture, can be made by boiling or extracting the seeds, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using them for medicinal purposes.
